  "findings": [
    "Vacuum decay test failed",
    "Micron level rose rapidly from 383 to 4167 microns in 10 minutes",
    "Indicates a significant leak in the system or severe moisture contamination"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Pressurize the system with dry nitrogen to locate the leak",
    "Perform soap bubble test or use an electronic leak detector on brazed joints",
    "Repair any identified leaks, re-test with nitrogen, and re-evacuate"
  ],
  "rationale": "A standing vacuum decay test should hold below 500 microns (or at least below 1000 microns depending on manufacturer specifications) without rising continuously. A rapid rise from 383 microns to 4167 microns in 10 minutes indicates a leak in the system rather than simple moisture outgassing, which typically levels off.",
  "references": [
    "EPA Section 608 Evacuation Requirements",
    "Industry Standard Evacuation and Micron Decay Procedures"
  ]
}
